result:
# Introducing Smart Drafts

We've all been there: the cursor blinks, the page stays white, and the sharpest idea in your head refuses to become the first sentence. That friction — the quiet cost of starting — is what pushed us to build Smart Drafts. We kept hearing from you that the hardest part of writing isn't the writing; it's the blank page staring back. So we set out to replace that blankness with a running start.

Starting today, every new document offers a one-click outline tailored to what you're actually working on. Smart Drafts reads your document title and pulls light signals from your recent context — the project you've been in, the docs you've opened this week, the themes you tend to return to — and proposes a structured outline you can accept, tweak, or toss. Nothing is auto-filled, nothing is sent anywhere it shouldn't be, and you stay fully in the driver's seat. It's a scaffold, not a script: a thoughtful first move so you can focus on the thinking that only you can do.

This is just the first step. Next up, we're working on **custom voice training** — so the outlines (and eventually the drafts themselves) sound like *you*, not like a generic assistant. If you've been with us a while, you know we build slowly and sweat the details, and Smart Drafts is no exception. Give it a try on your next doc, and as always, tell us what works and what doesn't. We read every note.

# Introducing Smart Drafts

We've all been there: the cursor blinks, the page stays white, and the sharpest idea in your head refuses to become the first sentence. That friction — the quiet cost of starting — is what pushed us to build Smart Drafts. We kept hearing from you that the hardest part of writing isn't the writing; it's the blank page staring back. So we set out to replace that blankness with a running start.

Starting today, every new document offers a one-click outline tailored to what you're actually working on. Smart Drafts reads your document title and pulls light signals from your recent context — the project you've been in, the docs you've opened this week, the themes you tend to return to — and proposes a structured outline you can accept, tweak, or toss. Nothing is auto-filled, nothing is sent anywhere it shouldn't be, and you stay fully in the driver's seat. It's a scaffold, not a script: a thoughtful first move so you can focus on the thinking that only you can do.

This is just the first step. Next up, we're working on **custom voice training** — so the outlines (and eventually the drafts themselves) sound like *you*, not like a generic assistant. If you've been with us a while, you know we build slowly and sweat the details, and Smart Drafts is no exception. Give it a try on your next doc, and as always, tell us what works and what doesn't. We read every note.
